column1: What is the lowest-cost, lowest-latency storage option for temporary data on EC2 that can be retrieved from elsewhere if lost?
column2: EC2 instance store (directly attached to instance, no additional cost, lowest latency)

column1: Can Amazon S3 be directly mounted to an EC2 instance?
column2: No, S3 cannot be directly mounted to an EC2 instance

column1: What fault-tolerance practice helps protect applications from a single point of failure in AWS?
column2: Launching instances in multiple availability zones within the same region

column1: What type of EBS volume is designed to deliver expected performance when attached to EBS-optimized instances?
column2: Provisioned IOPS volumes

column1: What happens to Elastic IP addresses that are not associated with a running instance?
column2: A nominal charge is imposed for unassociated Elastic IP addresses

column1: What AWS service improves application performance by retrieving data from high-throughput, low-latency in-memory data stores?
column2: ElastiCache

column1: What is SQS?
column2: A messaging service (not a caching or performance service)

column1: What is SNS?
column2: A notification service (not a caching or performance service)

column1: What is CloudFront?
column2: A web distribution service that optimizes performance for GET request workloads and reduces direct S3 requests

column1: Why is ElastiCache ideal for handling session state in web applications?
column2: It allows you to abstract HTTP sessions from web servers using Redis and Memcached, improving performance

column1: What caching strategy only loads data into the cache when necessary?
column2: Lazy loading (avoids filling cache with unrequested data)

column1: What is a recommended Lambda best practice regarding code structure?
column2: Avoid using recursive code

column1: What is the optimal method for uploading large objects to Amazon S3?
column2: Multipart upload (enables uploading large objects in parts)

column1: How can you optimize EC2 instance boot time?
column2: Pre-bake dependencies into the base AMI so provisioning work doesn't have to be done on boot, and update AMIs periodically

column1: What AWS service identifies unused or underutilized resources and provides cost reduction recommendations?
column2: AWS Trusted Advisor

column1: What is AWS CloudTrail used for?
column2: Logging API calls (not cost optimization)

column1: What is Amazon Inspector?
column2: A security assessment service (not a cost optimization tool)

column1: How does enforcing mandatory tagging help with cost optimization?
column2: Allows tracking and managing resources by owner, environment, or project for cost allocation and optimization

column1: What AWS service can enforce tagging policies on resources?
column2: AWS Config rules

column1: What is a cost-effective Auto Scaling strategy that provides significant savings while maintaining availability?
column2: Auto Scaling Group with a mix of On-Demand and Spot Instances (Spot can cost as little as 10% of On-Demand prices)

column1: Why shouldn't you use Spot Instances exclusively for production applications?
column2: Risk of downtime if all Spot Instances are terminated simultaneously

column1: What AWS feature automatically transitions S3 data between storage classes based on access patterns?
column2: S3 lifecycle policies

column1: Is S3 Standard appropriate for infrequently accessed data?
column2: No, S3 Standard is for commonly used data with no particular discount

column1: Is S3 Glacier appropriate for infrequently accessed data?
column2: No, S3 Glacier is only suitable for archival purposes, not merely infrequent access

column1: What AWS tool provides a detailed breakdown of your bill and shows cost changes over time?
column2: AWS Cost Explorer

column1: What do AWS Budgets help you do?
column2: Set alerts to avoid being caught off-guard by unexpected charges

column1: What do AWS Config rules help with regarding cost management?
column2: Monitor compliance with predefined rules to catch unexpected changes that may affect costs

column1: What type of Savings Plan offers up to 66% cost savings with flexibility across EC2, Fargate, and Lambda?
column2: Compute Savings Plans (flexible across instance families, types, and regions)

column1: Do Convertible Reserved Instances extend to ECS or Lambda?
column2: No, they only apply to EC2 instances (not ECS deployments or Lambda)

column1: What is the main limitation of Spot Instances?
column2: They are not guaranteed and are best for fault-tolerant tasks only

column1: What is the trade-off of using On-Demand Instances?
column2: They offer flexibility but at a higher cost compared to Savings Plans

column1: Why should you avoid certain HTTP headers in CloudFront requests?
column2: Headers like Date or User-Agent reduce cache hit ratio, increasing load on origin and reducing performance

column1: Where should bootstrapping configuration be placed for EC2 instances?
column2: In instance user data (not instance metadata)

column1: What is the benefit of using CloudFront with S3 for GET-heavy workloads?
column2: Optimizes performance and reduces direct S3 requests, which lowers costs
